pi_items.source: Task instructions require saving a screenshot to this exact path. Verified as a standard home desktop location consistent with the task environment using the 'ga' user account established in env.json. The path is contextually appropriate for a GUI task requiring screenshot evidence.

Verified via multiple sources: (1) env.json observation resolution [1920, 1080], (2) env.json recording.video_resolution [1920, 1080], (3) 1920 pixels is the standard width for Full HD (1080p) resolution per https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1080p and https://www.viewsonic.com/library/tech/monitor-resolution-aspect-ratio/

Verified via multiple sources: (1) env.json observation resolution [1920, 1080], (2) env.json recording.video_resolution [1920, 1080], (3) 1080 pixels is the standard height for Full HD (1080p) resolution per https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1080p

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating an ergonomic dashboard layout in OpenICE (Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment), a real medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital (verified at https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp and https://www.openice.info/). The task arranges three components: a Vital Signs clinical application, a simulated Infusion Pump device adapter, and a simulated Multiparameter Monitor device adapter in a T-Layout on a 1920x1080 Full HD display. The screenshot must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/dashboard_layout.png. All medical device data is synthetic, generated by OpenICE's simulated device adapters, not real patient data. The screen dimensions (1920x1080) are standard Full HD resolution verified against the environment configuration.
